behavior_summary: U8P_HAMOSTR::look is a simple 'U8P_HAMOSTR::look' (examine) process handler that displays a short bark text "Bone Crusher " and then suspends. It serves to show the item's name/short description to the player.
There are no conditionals, menus, helper calls, or flag reads/writes in this script; the behavior is unconditional and deterministic.

Item metadata (baseDamage 11, damageModifier 9, damage types [blunt, magic], isSpecial = true) is present as inferred object properties but is not modified or referenced by the U8P_HAMOSTR::look process itself.
notable_control_flow: U8P_HAMOSTR::look: unconditional sequence — emit a Bark with the text "Bone Crusher " then call suspend/terminate for the process.
No branching, loops, or external interactions; the function exits after presenting the bark.
open_questions: Why does the extracted artifact include item property metadata (damage, modifiers, types)? These properties appear to be object metadata not used by the U8P_HAMOSTR::look handler — is there a separate equip/use script that consumes them?
The bark text includes a trailing space ("Bone Crusher "). Is that intentional (formatting/prefixing in UI) or an extraction artifact?
Are there other processes (use, equip, or runtime scripts) for this item elsewhere in the game data that use the item's damage metadata?

main_function.name: U8P_HAMOSTR::look
main_function.purpose: Present the item's short name/description to the player when examined and then suspend the U8P_HAMOSTR::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine's U8P_HAMOSTR::look/examine system for this item (processType 0x0208).
Tied to player 'U8P_HAMOSTR::look' or examine action on the object; no additional in-script gating or flags.

function_map.name: U8P_HAMOSTR::look
function_map.role: Handle player examine/U8P_HAMOSTR::look action — emit short descriptive bark then suspend the process.
